decisions.observable_behavior: Every public request derives one Tenant-bound Security Principal from the Client API Key; foreign and unknown resources are non-enumerating.
decisions.failure_semantics: Authentication fails as 401-class, same-Tenant missing scope fails as 403-class, and foreign/unknown/deleted resources fail as indistinguishable 404-class outcomes before protected effects.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents cross-Tenant data, credential, account, job, asset, capability, and routing access and confused-deputy execution.

decisions.observable_behavior: Each Provider Account keeps one immutable Auth Mode whose allowed, prohibited, experimental, or gated risk state is evaluated independently from capability and health.
decisions.failure_semantics: Prohibited, killed, or gated-without-acknowledgement modes fail closed before credential use or Provider execution; reopening requires the documented evidence and operator gates.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents capability probes or routing policy from overriding compliance, acceptable-use, credential-custody, or kill-switch decisions.

decisions.observable_behavior: Client API Keys are one-time bearer credentials with Tenant-bound scopes; admission evaluates scope, request size, rate, concurrency, and quota in the locked order.
decisions.failure_semantics: Revoked or invalid keys fail at authentication; scope, size, rate, concurrency, quota, and dependency failures remain admission outcomes and never masquerade as Provider runtime failures.
decisions.security_impact: Enforces least privilege, immediate revoke behavior, anti-abuse isolation, fail-closed limit state, and non-secret key storage.

decisions.observable_behavior: Provider Accounts follow the locked create, submit, validate, probe, activate, refresh, reauthenticate, disable, revoke, and delete journeys with monotonic credential versions.
decisions.failure_semantics: An account is non-usable unless every usability gate passes for the current credential version; failures stop before Adapter execution and map to canonical remediation.
decisions.security_impact: Keeps Web and OAuth credentials independent, prevents stale or pending credentials from executing, and ensures delete/revoke removes use before metadata disappearance.

decisions.observable_behavior: Every account exposes a credential-version-bound Capability Snapshot with per-operation and observed-model status, provenance, freshness, and probe identity.
decisions.failure_semantics: Only fresh verified or conditionally_supported operation-model pairs are offerable; unsupported, unverified, stale, or invalid facts reject before upstream execution.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents static catalogs, another account's evidence, or a successful unrelated probe from authorizing unavailable or unsafe work.

decisions.observable_behavior: Routing constructs a same-Tenant candidate set and applies explicit selection, lease, affinity, policy, then declared fallback precedence.
decisions.failure_semantics: Missing policy, explicit pins, non-offerable capability, unusable accounts, prohibited risk, or insufficient non-commit proof fail closed without silent or cross-Tenant fallback.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents shared account pools, cross-mode widening, retry multiplication, and routing around lifecycle, risk, capability, or health gates.

decisions.observable_behavior: Chat runs through the locked X1-X6 lifecycle; streams emit open, zero or more delta/heartbeat events, then exactly one completed, failed, or canceled terminal.
decisions.failure_semantics: Cancel, disconnect, timeout, residual work, commit uncertainty, and idempotency no-steal retain one execution identity and conservative accounting without duplicate terminals or retries.
decisions.security_impact: Keeps Tenant/key occupancy attached to residual work, prevents replacement execution after uncertain commit, and avoids Provider-specific leakage in stream semantics.

decisions.observable_behavior: Assets are immutable Tenant-owned input, mask, or output objects with canonical decode/dimension/mask validation, bounded retention, idempotent deletion, and atomic storage reservation.
decisions.failure_semantics: Invalid media or dimensions fail before upstream; foreign and unknown IDs are non-enumerating; expired or deleted content is non-retrievable and capacity reservations release once.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents cross-Tenant content disclosure, mask downgrades, cap races, unbounded retention, and output visibility beyond the owner Tenant.

decisions.observable_behavior: Image operations create durable jobs with leases, fencing, one attempt ledger, commit certainty, immutable result manifests, cancellation, and placement-key output delivery.
decisions.failure_semantics: Committed or unknown attempts may recover only the same attempt; output retry reuses the manifest and never re-renders; cancellation and accounting settle conservatively.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents duplicate costly side effects, stale-worker commits, cross-Tenant recovery, optimistic refunds, and content leakage through staging or output placement.

decisions.observable_behavior: Credentials and Tenant-confidential content use separate encrypted, purpose-bound, Tenant/resource-bound access paths with independent business, crypto, and hash versions.
decisions.failure_semantics: Authorization, audit, key, binding, retention, or dependency failures deny decrypt/use; logical deletion immediately removes public/use authority even when encrypted evidence is held.
decisions.security_impact: Keeps plaintext out of application code and ordinary records, blocks handle-as-authority and cross-Tenant ciphertext reuse, and makes compromise/revocation/purge observable.

decisions.observable_behavior: All layers normalize to one Provider-independent error envelope, commit certainty, retryability, remediation, and exactly one retry owner per operation.
decisions.failure_semantics: Fingerprint conflict, in-progress, and uncertain claims never steal ownership; transport, Adapter, queue, and worker layers cannot create a second full-operation retry chain.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents duplicate side effects and diagnostic leakage while preserving non-enumeration, safe correlation, and honest admission-versus-runtime classification.

decisions.observable_behavior: Scoped health conditions, durable cooldowns, recovery permits, surface circuits, drain, quarantine, and Tenant disable remain independent from lifecycle and risk state.
decisions.failure_semantics: Stale observations cannot clear newer failures; cooldown expiry opens bounded half-open proof rather than marking healthy; quarantine and Auth Mode kill dominate routing without rewriting account health.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents upstream hammering, stale-success recovery, Tenant circuit poisoning, unsafe operator release, and health state from becoming authorization.

decisions.observable_behavior: The sole stable client contract is OpenAPI 3.1.1 `/v1` version 1.0.0 with 26 operations, exact scopes, operation-specific idempotency classes, and a frozen compatibility baseline.
decisions.failure_semantics: Breaking v1 changes, scope/auth changes, narrowed requests, closed-response expansion, or weakened idempotency fail validation and require a new major; deprecation requires the locked notice window and successor.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents silent authorization or replay-policy drift and requires future behavioral proof through the public HTTP surface and real composition.

decisions.observable_behavior: The Gateway is a Pure-Go module with domain, application, ports, HTTP transport, Provider adapters, Vault, persistence, jobs, observability, composition, and contract-test boundaries.
decisions.failure_semantics: Forbidden imports, secret-returning Vault APIs, generic retry/store shortcuts, private test seams, or unapproved dependency slots block implementation acceptance.
decisions.security_impact: Keeps HTTP, Provider protocol, queue, storage, and secret representations outside the canonical domain and makes production/test wiring share one composition root.

decisions.observable_behavior: Grok xAI OAuth chat and streaming use cli_chat_proxy, image generation and edit use api_x_ai, and inpaint remains unsupported; clients cannot select or override these surfaces.
decisions.failure_semantics: Activation chat proof does not authorize image operations; a missing or failed exact-surface capability probe rejects before execution, and no Adapter, routing, retry, or recovery path attempts the alternate surface.
decisions.security_impact: Prevents client-controlled upstream widening, cross-surface capability inflation, and silent fallback that could change credential, commit, or retry semantics.

implementation_slices.proof_seam: Production composition constructor exposed through public HTTP plus exported worker lifecycle, with compile-time dependency checks.
Public HTTP requests with controlled Principal, Admission, Replay, Audit, Clock, and ID ports.
Public management HTTP lifecycle with controlled AccountStore, protected CredentialVault use, Audit, and no plaintext observations.
Public model, account, capability, and routing HTTP operations plus controlled probe/recovery Adapter capabilities.
Public Asset and Render Job HTTP plus exported JobExecutor with controlled stores, JobRuntime, Vault, Render Adapter, Clock, and IDs.
Public non-streaming and SSE HTTP through real composition with controlled Chat Adapter, cancellation, residual tracking, Replay, and accounting.
All 26 stable operations through production composition, followed by per-Auth-Mode controlled and live probes without status inflation.

deferred_items.reason: The domain requires logical atomicity but current evidence does not justify a specific physical database driver or schema.
dependencies: implementation issue #42 foundation and persistence benchmark story
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before the first durable persistence implementation is selected; require cgo-free Pure-Go evidence, migration proof, transaction semantics, and no ORM/query builder.

deferred_items.reason: Logical envelope, AAD, versioning, and fail-closed behavior are locked, but KMS/HSM vendor and exact approved primitive depend on deployment custody requirements.
dependencies: implementation issue #42 Provider Account and Vault slice
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before production credential storage; record the primitive, key custody, cryptoperiod, recovery, and whether standard library crypto is insufficient.

deferred_items.reason: JobRuntime semantics are locked but no deployment evidence currently requires an external queue client.
dependencies: implementation issue #42 Asset and Render slice
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before multi-process durable delivery; document acknowledgement, redelivery, recovery, operational ownership, and why the standard library or selected database is insufficient.

deferred_items.reason: Single-region versus distributed coordination is an operational topology choice, not a missing domain or execution rule.
dependencies: measured load, availability, and recovery evidence
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before production deployment or when one-process/single-region recovery cannot meet an accepted SLO.

deferred_items.reason: SLO thresholds, alerts, canary percentages, and launch gates require a running composition and Provider measurements.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: After runtime conformance and before any production Tenant or Provider credential is admitted.

deferred_items.reason: Legacy backend and Photoshop migration must consume a stable running Gateway rather than change its locked Public API semantics.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When the Gateway `/v1` conformance suite is green in the target deployment and the replacement Plugin story is ready.

deferred_items.reason: Named TTL, retention, cooldown, lease, timeout, quota, and retry classes are locked while many numeric values remain operationally tunable.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When observed safety, latency, cost, or Provider behavior shows a named default does not meet its stated invariant; semantics and fail-closed rules may not be weakened.

deferred_items.reason: Probe operations must be bounded and non-hammering, but exact per-Tenant budgets require live Provider cost and throttle evidence.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before enabling scheduled or user-triggered probes in production.

deferred_items.reason: The accepted MVP rejects stale snapshots; a grace window could weaken pre-execution capability proof.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Only if measured probe outages create unacceptable availability and a per-operation low-risk grace policy can be proven fail-closed for unsupported or invalid facts.

deferred_items.reason: Immediate Client API Key invalidation on rotation is the accepted MVP and avoids dual-valid bearer ambiguity.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When a concrete client rollout requires overlap and bounded dual-key admission can preserve revoke propagation and audit identity.

deferred_items.reason: Provider credential cutover occurs only after the pending version probes successfully; longer dual-valid grace increases secret exposure.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When a Provider proves atomic cutover is operationally unsafe and a bounded, version-fenced overlap is specified.

deferred_items.reason: Codex API-key login is not one of the six accepted Auth Modes and cannot be merged into Codex OAuth without changing credential and risk semantics.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When product scope explicitly requests the mode and evidence establishes its independent credential lifecycle, risk envelope, and capability surface.

deferred_items.reason: The accepted routing model does not authorize pooled multi-account load balancing beyond deterministic Tenant-owned candidates.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When a Tenant-facing requirement needs concurrent pool balancing and can preserve explicit selection, leases, quotas, and no cross-Tenant sharing.

deferred_items.reason: Fallback is opt-in and fail-closed; automatic fallback would change observable execution and retry behavior.
dependencies: new Public API and routing decision
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When product explicitly requests default failover and every operation has authoritative no-commit or idempotent recovery proof.

deferred_items.reason: Cross-Auth-Mode fallback is allowed only through an explicit Tenant policy list and never as a default.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When product requests a default cross-mode policy and user disclosure, risk acknowledgements, capability equality, and retry safety are specified.

deferred_items.reason: The stable MVP locks text chat ordering while tool calling and richer multimodal streaming remain secondary unverified operations.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When a client requirement names tool or multimodal semantics and at least one Auth Mode has probe-backed support without breaking terminal ordering.

deferred_items.reason: Disconnect is implicit cancel and the accepted MVP has no client-visible stream resume; server-side residual recovery remains separate.
dependencies: new chat execution and API decision
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When clients require resume and a cursor/idempotency contract can prove one execution, one terminal, bounded retention, and Tenant isolation.

deferred_items.reason: Current recovery is server-side job/attempt recovery, not resumable client upload or download transport.
dependencies: new Asset and Public API decision
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When measured transfer sizes or reliability require a client resume protocol with bounded storage, ownership, and integrity semantics.

deferred_items.reason: Gateway correctness cannot assume Provider-native idempotency, although an Adapter may use it to strengthen same-attempt recovery.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When an upstream publishes and a live probe confirms a stable idempotency key contract for the exact Auth Mode and operation.

deferred_items.reason: Expired outputs are never implicitly re-rendered because that would create a new costly side effect under an old job identity.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When product requires recreation and defines it as a new admitted request with new quota, idempotency, and output lifetime.

deferred_items.reason: MVP upload is a bounded single request; chunking adds partial-object, retention, integrity, and admission semantics.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When required input sizes exceed the accepted single-request limit and a resumable ownership/integrity protocol is specified.

deferred_items.reason: Dedupe is an optimization and must not create cross-Tenant metadata, ciphertext, retention, or deletion coupling.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When measured storage cost justifies implementation and dedupe remains Tenant-local or otherwise proves complete cryptographic and lifecycle isolation.

deferred_items.reason: MVP does not retain a user-visible transcript by default; replay/recovery content has a separate bounded confidential lifecycle.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When product explicitly requires history and defines consent, retrieval, deletion, retention, export, and Tenant administration.

deferred_items.reason: Operator decrypt is default-deny and would materially change the security boundary.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When a mandatory incident workflow cannot be met without access and dual control, time bounds, purpose binding, notification, and audit-before-decrypt are specified.

deferred_items.reason: Resource-level ownership and deletion are locked, but full Tenant export/deletion orchestration is a separate product and compliance workflow.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before offering Tenant account deletion/export or when a jurisdictional obligation requires it.

deferred_items.reason: Jurisdiction-specific schedules and attestations require legal/compliance input; the current contract already requires bounded retention and non-use after logical deletion.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before processing production data in a jurisdiction with specific retention, deletion, export, or evidence-hold requirements.

deferred_items.reason: Ordinary records cannot retain raw upstream payloads; authorized incident evidence needs a separate encrypted and access-controlled lifecycle.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When incident response requires raw evidence that cannot be represented by the existing safe diagnostic allowlist.

deferred_items.reason: The stable API defines individual operations; a batch wrapper could accidentally multiply retry owners and commit boundaries.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When a concrete batch use case is requested and each contained operation retains its own idempotency, retry owner, accounting, and terminal result.

deferred_items.reason: Current quotas are admission and abuse controls, not invoices or commercial metering.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When the product explicitly requires billing, credits, refunds, plans, or invoice-grade usage semantics.

deferred_items.reason: The accepted MVP contains six Auth Modes and excludes additional Provider families and official API adapters.
dependencies: capability evidence and stable API fit
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When product scope names an additional Provider/Auth Mode and its ownership, credential, risk, capability, error, and retry contracts are specified.

deferred_items.reason: It is unresolved whether acting as a technical agent of the account owner mitigates Provider credential-sharing restrictions.
dependencies: written counsel analysis for target launch jurisdictions
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before promoting any Auth Mode to allowed, or before promoting an experimental Web mode to gated on this theory.

deferred_items.reason: Reverse-engineering restrictions and their enforceability vary by jurisdiction and can change the permitted launch envelope for Web modes.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before launching an experimental Web Auth Mode outside a pure lab in a target region.

deferred_items.reason: OpenAI permission for third-party SaaS custody of Codex OAuth tokens outside official clients remains unresolved.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before promoting ChatGPT Codex OAuth from gated to allowed.

deferred_items.reason: Product-specific Antigravity terms beyond the general Google API and Gemini developer-family terms have not been established.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before promoting Gemini Antigravity OAuth from gated to allowed.

deferred_items.reason: The exact xAI-versus-X issuer and governing-terms matrix for Grok Web SSO tokens remains incomplete.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before any future change from the prohibited Grok Web SSO risk state.

deferred_items.reason: The scope of xAI's competitive-product clause against a multi-provider SaaS Gateway remains unresolved.
dependencies: counsel analysis of the product shape
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before promoting Grok xAI OAuth from gated to allowed.

deferred_items.reason: The commercial fee structure has not been characterized against resale, lease, or time-sharing restrictions.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before making resale-style marketing claims or promoting a gated mode to allowed where commercial characterization matters.

deferred_items.reason: Production launch geography can change applicable enforcement and compliance risk even when the technical contract is unchanged.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Before selecting or expanding production launch regions.

deferred_items.reason: Asset byte/count cap semantics are locked, while their numeric defaults and reclamation timing remain operationally tunable.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When measured Tenant usage or recovery behavior justifies changing cap numbers without weakening atomic committed-plus-reserved accounting.

deferred_items.reason: The stable wire encoding is locked; reopening is needed only if a requested change alters canonical error semantics rather than representation.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When a client requirement cannot be represented by the current canonical code, retryability, remediation, and safe-context contract.

deferred_items.reason: Automatic re-execution after uncertain or committed work is forbidden because it can duplicate non-idempotent side effects.
dependencies: explicit product, billing, and commit-proof decision
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Only when product explicitly accepts at-least-once execution and defines client-visible duplicate, accounting, and reconciliation semantics.

deferred_items.reason: The logical Tenant/resource-bound key hierarchy is fixed, but a future architecture may require a different equivalent KMS/HSM topology.
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: When the selected deployment or custody model cannot implement the accepted logical hierarchy without a topology change.

deferred_items.reason: The accepted rule forbids keeping revoked Provider Credential material decryptable under a retention hold.
dependencies: binding legal requirement and security ADR
deferred_items.reopen_trigger: Only when a binding legal requirement would change the no-decryptable-credential-hold rule and an ADR defines equivalent non-use and access controls.
